meaning: n.典狱官,看守人,学监,区长,(供煮食的)一种冬梨
examples: If Jan had gone to the officers she trusted, they would have had to repeat her story to the same warden.
He was a warden at the local parish church.
The letter closed with sincere greetings which I did not think referred to me, but the warden was sincere about the issue.
Eleven buses a day, including 10 double-deckers, ferry children to the Warden Hill Road school.
Animal warden Sue Knight used a microchip detector over Diesel's skin and found Ms Chapman's details.
